blocks.content: They went downstairs. Dean turned on every light.

The kitchen looked the same as it had two hours ago — dishes drying on the rack, a half-full bottle of wine on the counter, the plate Cade had set aside for Marcus still covered in foil on the stove. The ordinary cruelty of a house that doesn't know yet.
They went out the back. The porch. The ocean was there, same as always, doing its indifferent thing.

blocks.speaker: Cade
blocks.content: He said he'd be down by six.

blocks.speaker: Cade
blocks.content: He said he was fine. I looked right at him and he said he was fine.

blocks.speaker: Cade
blocks.content: I should have gone back up. After dinner. When he didn't come down I should have gone up earlier.

blocks.speaker: Cade
blocks.content: That's not— that doesn't help, Dean. That's not the thing to say right now.
Dean closed his mouth. He knew Cade was right. He sat down on the porch step and put his forearms on his knees and looked at the ground.
Sophie was crying quietly. Wren sat beside her and didn't say anything and that was the right thing to do.
Nadia stood at the railing, looking out at the water. Her back to all of them.
